motto: Let's do the obvious: Asking seniors about their last final exams, Studying the topics that appear in the exams, Spending time on what actually matters. Start this semester with a blast!

roadmap.title: Guidance with experience
roadmap.subtitle: Fresh Senior Insights
roadmap.summary: Connect with immediate seniors who have just cleared the same courses.
roadmap.description: Immediate seniors who just finished the final exams have the freshest memory of what was taught, how the instructor graded, what resources were actually useful, and what traps to avoid. Talking to them gives you a realistic, head-start strategy for the semester.

roadmap.title: Reverse Engineering Questions
roadmap.subtitle: Exam-First Analysis
roadmap.summary: Analyze past exam papers to isolate high-yield recurring topics.
roadmap.description: Instead of studying the entire textbook line-by-line, we look at the last few final and midterm exam papers first. By mapping questions back to syllabus topics, we identify recurring patterns and high-yield concepts that are almost guaranteed to appear.

roadmap.title: Brainstorming & Collaboration
roadmap.subtitle: Co-created Solutions
roadmap.summary: Solve past papers together and share detailed study guides.
roadmap.description: Students compile their notes, draft code, and write step-by-step solutions to exam questions in shared repositories or forums. Peer discussion groups help clarify complex edge cases and ensure everyone is aligned before exam week.

roadmap.title: Time Management
roadmap.subtitle: Strategic Efficiency
roadmap.summary: Optimize study schedules to minimize academic overhead.
roadmap.description: By focusing primarily on high-probability exam topics first, students can drastically reduce the number of hours spent on aimless studying. This targeted workflow prevents last-minute panic and keeps stress levels manageable.

roadmap.title: ECA, Skill dev, Job Prep
roadmap.subtitle: Real-World Focus
roadmap.summary: Redirect saved academic hours to build career-ready skills.
roadmap.description: The ultimate goal is to minimize waste in pure academic cramming so students have ample time for Extracurricular Activities (ECAs), building software projects, learning marketable skills, participating in hackathons, and preparing for job interviews.

resources.description: Central repositories and archives collected by UITS students.

items.title: UITS Exam Repository
items.description: A centralized archive of past mid and final exam question papers collected by UITS students.

items.title: CSE Lecture Notes Drive
items.description: Curated Google Drive folders containing course slides, lab codes, and student notebooks.

resources.description: Boilerplates and toolkits to excel in tech contests and projects.

items.title: oU1TS Hackathon Survival Kit
items.description: Templates, boilerplate codes, and guidelines for rapid prototyping in web, mobile, and AI contests.

items.title: Interactive Career Roadmaps
items.description: Role-based career roadmaps and educational guides to help you pick your professional track.

about.title: Our Structural Vision
about.description: UITS Course Mastery is an open-source, student-centered initiative designed to optimize academic traversal. Traditional university curriculums often demand extensive rote memorization of low-impact topics, leading to high student burnout and insufficient time for building real-world software engineering or research skills. By establishing an organized mentorship bridge between recently promoted seniors and incoming juniors, we bypass these academic bottlenecks.

features.title: Reverse-Engineering Exam Vectors
features.description: We analyze past exam structures to isolate major topic distributions. This ensures students study what actually matters, saving time while still excelling academically.

features.title: Fresh Peer-Led Mentorship
features.description: Immediate seniors share practical course tips, instructor expectations, grading criteria, and curated resources rather than repeating dry textbook theory.

features.title: Open Source Collaboration
features.description: All notes, solution guides, and lecture recordings are free, public, and open-source, hosted under the oU1TS Community banner.

instructions.title: Join the Facebook Group
instructions.description: This is our primary community portal. Request entry using your UITS Student ID to ask questions, request notes, and coordinate peer reviews.

instructions.title: Verify Student Status
instructions.description: To maintain student privacy and protect shared academic assets, verify your status with a maintainer. The project is not intended to be restrictive; rather, these verification procedures are necessary to ensure a private study environment where students can comfortably share chat histories, class notes, and solutions.

instructions.title: Save Contact & Join
instructions.description: Once verified, add the maintainer's phone number to your contacts before joining the group. WhatsApp security protocol only synchronizes and shares group chat history to contacts saved in your address book upon adding you.
